---
title: "can't open software center"
date: 2013-01-03
forum: New to Ubuntu
---

### Post by Civetcat on 2013-01-03
Hi 

I've just installed ubuntu 12.04 and it was working fine till I tried to install skype.  Now when I try to open the software center, I can't.  I get the following message:
The application Ubuntu Software has closed unexpectedly. 
'Executable path
    /usr/share/software-center/update-software-center-agent'
[IMG]http://ubuntuforums.org/data:image/png;base64,[/IMG] 
And a further message:
'Could not determine the package or source package name.'
[IMG]http://ubuntuforums.org/data:image/png;base64,[/IMG]
I've searched the forums and tried a few things but nothing has worked.  Do I have to reinstall ubuntu or is there something I can do to solve this?

I would appreciate if anyone can help, as I can't seem to solve this problem.

Thanks!!

---

### Post by RottNKorpse on 2013-01-03
Did you install Skype from the Software Center or from the Skype website?

The first thing I would suggest is to remove skype and see if the Software Center will load up again.

1. open the Dash
2. type in "Terminal"
OR
1. Keyboard Shortcut = Ctrl + Alt + T

Use the following command in the terminal:
```
sudo apt-get autoremove --purge skype
```

and then
```
mv .Skype .Skype.bak
```

to remove the working folder but to keep it as a backup (bak) folder. ~/.Skype folder in your home is where  everything related to your skype subscription is stored locally on your  computer.


This information is also available on the following thread: "**How can I uninstall Skype completely?"**
[http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=1695589](http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=1695589)

---

### Post by Civetcat on 2013-01-04
Thanks for that.  I did all that but it seems that is not the problem.  It seems skype had not installed after all.

---

### Post by NikTh on 2013-01-04
Try the commands below.. one line at time 
Copy-Paste from here ! Do not make mistake. 
```
sudo apt-get remove --purge software-center 
sudo rm -r /usr/share/software-center/
rm -r ~/.cache/software-center/
sudo apt-get install software-center ubuntu-desktop
```Thanks

---

### Post by ibjsb4 on 2013-01-05
If still having trouble

```
sudo apt-get update
```

And please post

---

### Post by Bucky Ball on 2013-01-05
How did you attempt to install Skype? Through Software Centre? 

Uninstalling the Software Centre and reinstalling is a radical solution they may not be necessary as you could first just try uninstalling Skype, seeing if everything goes back to normal, and starting again. That way you have a chance to work out what's happening when you reinstall.

---

### Post by Civetcat on 2013-01-08
> **NikTh said:**
> Try the commands below.. one line at time 
Copy-Paste from here ! Do not make mistake. 
```
sudo apt-get remove --purge software-center 
sudo rm -r /usr/share/software-center/
rm -r ~/.cache/software-center/
sudo apt-get install software-center ubuntu-desktop
```Thanks

Thanks.  I've done all of this, one line at a time.
When I entered line 1 it indicated that it was removing ubuntu-desktop and software-center and processing various things and then ended with:
E: malformed line 57 in source list /etc/apt/sources.list (dist parse)
E: the list of sources could not be read
I then entered line 2 but it says command not found.

I then entered line 3 and it reads:
cannot remove '/home/.gvfs/': Device or resource busy
cannot remove '/home/.config/': Directory not empty
cannot remove '.cache/software-center/': No such file or directoryI then entered line 4 and after reading package lists, building dependency tree, it reads:
Package software-center is not available, but is referred to by another package
This may mean that the package is missing, has been obsoleted, or is only available from another source
E: Package 'software-center' has no installation candidate
E: Unable to locate package ubuntu-desktop
I then tried sudo apt-get update and it reads:
E: Malformed line 57 in source list /etc/apt/sources.list (dist parse)
E: The list of sources could not be read.
Does anyone know what is wrong?  It seems now I've uninstalled the software-center but it doesn't want to reinstall.

Thanks again!

---

Did you install Skype through Software Centre?

I didn't install it through software center.  I used a link I found on google because it said that the version of skype of the software center was missing something.  

The thing is, I don't think skype even installed but it somehow corrupted the software center.

deb [http://archive.canonical.com/](http://archive.canonical.com/) partner

I'm not sure how to delete the skype repository.

### Post by Bashing-om on 2013-01-09
Civetcat; Hi !

Are you comfortable editing files ?

Make a backup of /etc/apt/sources.list
```
 cp /etc/apt/sources.list /etc/apt/sources.list-bac
```Then edit the file line to make it this: 
> deb [http://archive.canonical.com/ubuntu](http://archive.canonical.com/ubuntu) precise partner-the directive "ubuntu precise" is missing-

That I expect will fix ya up. 
[INDENT][INDENT]just ry'n to help <== BDQ

[/INDENT][/INDENT]

---

### Post by Bucky Ball on 2013-01-10
> **Bashing-om said:**
> 
```
 cp /etc/apt/sources.list etc/apt/sources.list-bac
```

+1. But perhaps:

```
sudo cp /etc/apt/sources.list /etc/apt/sources.list-bac
```... and if you screw up with your tweaking:

```
sudo cp /etc/apt/sources.list-bac /etc/apt/sources.list
```

@ Bashing-om: Needs /etc/ rather than etc/. ;)

Make a backup of /etc/apt/sources.list

 cp /etc/apt/sources.list /etc/apt/sources.list-bac

-the directive "ubuntu precise" is missing-

That I expect will fix ya up. [INDENT][INDENT]just ry'n to help <== BDQ

Hi Bashing-om

I tried to edit the file but it won't let me save it.  I get an error message saying I don't have rights to save over the file!

---

### Post by Bashing-om on 2013-01-14
Civetcat;

Altering a file not in your home directory requires admin authority.
Try this:
```
gksudo gedit /etc/apt/sources.list
``` "gksudo" is the graphical applier to SuperUser Do aka '"sudo"
[INDENT]a bit of learning, aye ?<== BDQ

[/INDENT]

---

### Post by Bucky Ball on 2013-01-15
Or just use:

```
sudo nano /etc/apt/sources.list
```... and don't both with Gedit's GUI. Before tweaking much (or possibly at all) I would advise:

```
sudo cp /etc/apt/sources.list /etc/apt/sources.list.bak
```... to make a backup of the file in case your tweaks break something. If they do, simply:

```
sudo mv /etc/apt/sources.list.bak /etc/apt/sources.list
```... to put the original back where it was. ;)

---

### Post by Civetcat on 2013-02-12
> **NikTh said:**
> Try the commands below.. one line at time 
Copy-Paste from here ! Do not make mistake. 
```
sudo apt-get remove --purge software-center 
sudo rm -r /usr/share/software-center/
rm -r ~/.cache/software-center/
sudo apt-get install software-center ubuntu-desktop
```Thanks
 
Hi.  Do these commands to remove the software center also remove all other files saved on the ubuntu partition?  I seem to have lost all my files. Is there any way to recover them.  Help please!

---

### Post by Bashing-om on 2013-02-12
Civetcat;

The referenced commands will only affect the Software Center and the desktop config files.
Remember: ubuntu assumes you know what you are doing, and will happily comply with your commands. Did you do as NiHTh directed to "copy and past" the commands ? If not, using "sudo -r" there is no telling what was removed.

As to what to do about the lost other files ???????????
well, is there an older kernel available in the grub menu ? (new install, not likely) If so might try booting the older kernel, see if the missing files are present in that booted kernel. If so ---wait for another kernel update to become available.

else: (re)install ubuntu.

I know of no reliable way to restore arbitrary files deleted from the command line. One might closely examine the commands in the terminals history and perchance tediously rebuild --dependency hell ??--:: (re)install would be much quicker and surer.
[INDENT][INDENT]just try'n to help

[/INDENT][/INDENT]

---

A reinstall could find you in the same place as you are now so issue not resolved. ;)
